Keywords: onboarding, signup, setup, first time, welcome, activation, first value, getting started
Good Story Example: As a new user completing signup, I want to see a progress indicator during onboarding so that I know how many steps remain and don't abandon mid-flow.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want onboarding.
AC Example:
- [ ] Progress bar shows current step / total steps
- [ ] User can skip optional steps
- [ ] Completion triggers activation event in analytics
- Given the user is on step 3 of 5, when they complete the step, then the progress bar advances and the next step is shown
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: too large — split into (1) sign-up flow, (2) product tour, (3) first action
SaaS Context Notes: PLG: Onboarding is the conversion funnel. Time-to-first-value is the north star. Every required field and extra click hurts conversion.

Keywords: login, signin, auth, password, credential, sso, saml, oauth, mfa, two-factor
Good Story Example: As a returning user, I want to sign in with my company SSO so that I don't manage a separate password for this tool.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to login.
AC Example:
- [ ] SSO redirect sends user to IdP login page
- [ ] Successful auth returns user to their last visited page
- [ ] Failed auth shows specific error: 'SSO not configured for your domain'
- [ ] MFA prompt appears if enforced by admin policy
INVEST Issues to Watch: Independent: SSO and password login are separate stories
SaaS Context Notes: Enterprise: SSO/SAML is a deal-breaker requirement. Consumer: Magic links beat passwords for activation.

Keywords: password, reset, forgot, recover, account access, locked out, email link
Good Story Example: As a locked-out user, I want to securely reset my password via an email link so that I can regain access to my account without contacting support.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want a 'Forgot Password' button.
AC Example:
- [ ] Reset link sent within 30 seconds of request
- [ ] Link expires after 24 hours
- [ ] New password must meet security requirements (8+ chars, 1 number, 1 special)
- [ ] User is redirected to login after successful reset
- [ ] Error shown if email is not registered: 'No account found with that email'
INVEST Issues to Watch: Testable: all criteria are clearly verifiable
SaaS Context Notes: Support ticket deflector: poor password reset UX is the #1 support ticket category in SaaS.

Keywords: dashboard, home, overview, summary, key metrics, at a glance, quick view
Good Story Example: As a sales manager, I want to see my team's weekly performance at a glance on the dashboard so that I can identify who needs coaching without running multiple reports.
Bad Story Example: As the system, I need to display key metrics on a dashboard.
AC Example:
- Given the user is a sales manager, when they open the dashboard, then they see their team's activity metrics for the current week
- [ ] Data refreshes every 60 seconds or on demand
- [ ] Empty state shown with guidance CTA if no data exists
- [ ] Manager sees only their direct reports' data (RBAC enforced)
INVEST Issues to Watch: Valuable: tied to a business outcome (coaching decision); avoid building 'just a dashboard'
SaaS Context Notes: B2B: Dashboard is often the first screen after login — first impressions define retention. Always build the empty state.

Keywords: import, upload, csv, bulk, migrate, data entry, file
Good Story Example: As an operations manager, I want to import contacts via CSV so that I can migrate our CRM data in bulk instead of entering records one-by-one.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to upload a CSV file.
AC Example:
- [ ] Accepts CSV up to 50MB; rejects with message: 'File exceeds 50MB, please split'
- [ ] Validates column headers on upload; shows mapping UI for unrecognized columns
- [ ] Shows progress bar during import with row count
- [ ] Import completes in <5 min for 10,000 rows
- [ ] Shows summary: X imported, Y skipped (duplicates), Z errors with downloadable error log
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: needs spike on file parsing performance
SaaS Context Notes: Common edge cases: duplicate detection, encoding issues (UTF-8 vs Latin-1), semicolon delimiters, empty rows, missing required fields.

Keywords: export, download, csv, report, extract, share
Good Story Example: As a finance analyst, I want to export transaction data as CSV filtered by date range so that I can run analysis in Excel for the quarterly review.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to export data.
AC Example:
- [ ] User can select date range, columns, and file format (CSV/XLSX)
- [ ] Export of 100,000 rows completes in <60 seconds
- [ ] User receives email with download link if export takes >10 seconds
- [ ] Export respects user's RBAC — only exports data they have access to
- [ ] Download link expires after 48 hours
INVEST Issues to Watch: Independent: export format options (CSV/XLSX) can be a separate story
SaaS Context Notes: Compliance note: Exports of PII require audit logging in enterprise SaaS.

Keywords: billing, subscription, plan, upgrade, downgrade, payment, invoice, trial, renewal
Good Story Example: As an account owner, I want to upgrade my plan from Starter to Pro in self-serve so that I can unlock advanced features without contacting sales.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to change my plan.
AC Example:
- Given the user is on the Starter plan, when they click Upgrade, then they see the Pro plan details and pricing with a payment form
- [ ] Upgrade takes effect immediately after successful payment
- [ ] User receives email confirmation with invoice
- [ ] Prorated charge calculated correctly for mid-cycle upgrades
- [ ] Failed payment shows specific error and retry option (no silent failure)
INVEST Issues to Watch: Testable: payment edge cases (failed card, proration) must each have explicit AC
SaaS Context Notes: PLG: Upgrade friction kills revenue. Self-serve billing with proration is table stakes.

Keywords: team, invite, member, collaborate, share, permission, workspace, role
Good Story Example: As a workspace admin, I want to invite team members by email and assign roles so that I can control who can view versus edit our shared data.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to invite people.
AC Example:
- [ ] Admin can enter multiple emails (comma-separated) and assign role per email
- [ ] Invitee receives email within 2 minutes with accept link
- [ ] Invitee accepting creates their account and lands in the workspace
- [ ] Admin can remove, re-invite, or change roles at any time
- Given the invitee's email domain doesn't match SSO domain, when they accept, then they're prompted to set a password
INVEST Issues to Watch: Small: split into (1) invite flow, (2) role assignment, (3) removal/offboarding
SaaS Context Notes: B2B growth: invite flow is a viral loop. Friction here limits expansion revenue.

Keywords: settings, preferences, profile, account, notification, config, customize
Good Story Example: As a user, I want to configure my notification preferences so that I only receive alerts for events that require my attention, reducing inbox noise.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to manage settings.
AC Example:
- [ ] User can toggle each notification type independently (in-app, email, Slack)
- [ ] Settings save immediately without a separate save button
- [ ] User can set a quiet hours window for email notifications
- [ ] Changes take effect on the next qualifying event (not retroactively)
- [ ] Reset to defaults option available
INVEST Issues to Watch: Independent: profile settings and notification settings are separate stories
SaaS Context Notes: Settings are often deprioritized but directly impact retention — noisy notifications cause churn.

Keywords: notification, alert, email, push, in-app, bell, digest, real-time
Good Story Example: As a project contributor, I want to receive in-app notifications when someone comments on my task so that I can respond promptly without monitoring the tool constantly.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want notifications.
AC Example:
- [ ] In-app notification bell shows unread count badge
- [ ] Clicking notification navigates to the relevant context
- [ ] Email digest sent at user's configured frequency
- Given the user has opted out of email notifications for comments, when a comment is added, then no email is sent but the in-app notification still fires
INVEST Issues to Watch: Small: split by notification type (comments, mentions, status changes)
SaaS Context Notes: D7/D30 retention driver. Balance between helpful and spammy. Always build unsubscribe.

Keywords: search, filter, sort, find, query, lookup, browse, full-text
Good Story Example: As a customer success manager, I want to search and filter accounts by health score and renewal date so that I can prioritize at-risk accounts without manually scanning the list.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want to search.
AC Example:
- [ ] Search returns results within 500ms for queries against 1M records
- [ ] Filters can be combined (AND logic by default, OR optional)
- [ ] Active filters shown as removable chips
- [ ] Results update in real-time as filter values change
- [ ] Empty search results show helpful guidance and suggested terms
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: full-text search vs simple filter have very different complexity
SaaS Context Notes: Power users define their value through search. Poor search drives support tickets and churn.

Keywords: admin, user management, rbac, permission, role, deactivate, audit
Good Story Example: As an IT admin, I want to deactivate a user's account immediately when they leave the company so that their access is revoked before they can access any company data.
Bad Story Example: As an admin, I want to manage users.
AC Example:
- Given an admin deactivates a user, when the action is confirmed, then all active sessions for that user are invalidated within 60 seconds
- [ ] Deactivated user's data is preserved (not deleted) and transferable to another user
- [ ] Admin sees audit log entry for the deactivation action
- [ ] Deactivated user sees a clear message on their next login attempt
INVEST Issues to Watch: Independent: user deactivation is separate from role management and user invitation
SaaS Context Notes: Enterprise: offboarding speed is a security requirement. 60-second session invalidation is non-negotiable.

Keywords: integration, connect, webhook, api, sync, third-party, zapier, slack, salesforce, jira
Good Story Example: As a sales ops manager, I want to sync our tool's deal data to Salesforce automatically so that I don't maintain two separate systems of record.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want Salesforce integration.
AC Example:
- [ ] OAuth connection flow completes in <3 steps
- [ ] Sync runs automatically every 15 minutes or on-demand
- [ ] Sync errors shown in an integration health dashboard with retry option
- Given a Salesforce field is deleted, when sync runs, then the integration flags it as a configuration error (not silently drops data)
- [ ] User can map custom fields
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: needs spike on Salesforce API limits and data model mapping
SaaS Context Notes: Integration reliability is a trust issue. Silent sync failures destroy data integrity trust.

Keywords: ai, assist, suggest, automate, generate, recommend, predict, smart, intelligent
Good Story Example: As a product manager, I want the AI to suggest acceptance criteria for my user story based on the story description so that I can write comprehensive ACs faster without starting from scratch.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want AI to help.
AC Example:
- [ ] AI suggestion appears within 3 seconds of triggering
- [ ] User can accept all, accept individual items, or dismiss the suggestion
- [ ] User can edit any AI-generated content before saving
- Given the AI confidence is low, when generating suggestions, then the output is labeled 'low confidence — please review'
- [ ] All AI-generated content is stored with provenance metadata
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: needs spike on LLM latency and token cost; split into generation vs. refinement
SaaS Context Notes: AI features must have a clear fallback when confidence is low. Never replace user intent silently.

Keywords: report, analytics, chart, graph, trend, export, insight, visualization
Good Story Example: As an executive, I want a weekly summary report emailed to me every Monday so that I can review KPI trends without logging in to the tool.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want reports.
AC Example:
- [ ] Report generated at 8am user's local timezone every Monday
- [ ] Report includes delta vs prior week for each KPI
- [ ] User can configure which metrics appear in the report
- [ ] Email includes a 'View full report' link that deep-links to the in-product dashboard
- [ ] Report skipped if no new data was generated in the week
INVEST Issues to Watch: Small: scheduled reports vs ad-hoc reports vs exported reports are separate stories
SaaS Context Notes: B2B: executives use reports as the primary touchpoint. Digest emails drive retention and perceived value.

Keywords: empty, zero state, no data, first time, blank, placeholder
Good Story Example: As a new user who hasn't created any projects yet, I want to see a helpful empty state with a clear first action so that I know what to do next and don't feel lost.
Bad Story Example: (Often not written as a story — which is the problem)
AC Example:
- [ ] Empty state shows a brief explanation of what would appear here
- [ ] Primary CTA button guides the user to their first action
- [ ] Illustration or icon provides visual context (not just blank white space)
- [ ] Empty state is distinct from a loading state and an error state
INVEST Issues to Watch: Valuable: empty states directly impact activation rate for new users
SaaS Context Notes: The most commonly skipped UI state. Causes new users to churn before activating.

Keywords: error, failure, retry, fallback, undo, recover, broken, crash
Good Story Example: As a user who encounters an error during a form submission, I want to see a clear error message with a way to retry so that I don't lose my entered data and can resolve the issue myself.
Bad Story Example: (Often not written — handled ad hoc in dev)
AC Example:
- Given the API call fails, when the error occurs, then an inline error message appears explaining the issue in plain language
- [ ] Error message includes suggested action (retry, contact support, check docs)
- [ ] User's form data is preserved after an error
- [ ] Retry button triggers the same action without requiring re-entry
- [ ] Persistent errors after 3 retries prompt the user to contact support
INVEST Issues to Watch: Testable: must define specific error messages for each failure mode
SaaS Context Notes: Poor error handling generates support tickets and destroys trust. Each error message should be written like support copy.

Keywords: mobile, responsive, tablet, ios, android, touch, viewport
Good Story Example: As a field sales rep, I want to log a call note on my phone immediately after a meeting so that I capture accurate information while it's fresh, without waiting until I'm at my desk.
Bad Story Example: As a user, I want a mobile view.
AC Example:
- [ ] Core workflows complete on 375px viewport without horizontal scroll
- [ ] Touch targets are minimum 44x44px (WCAG 2.5.5)
- [ ] Form inputs don't zoom in on focus (font-size 16px minimum)
- [ ] Critical actions accessible within 1 thumb-reach on a standard phone
- [ ] Offline: unsaved form data persisted locally and synced on reconnect
INVEST Issues to Watch: Independent: mobile layout is separate from native app stories
SaaS Context Notes: Responsive is table stakes for PLG — signup and onboarding flows are frequently started on mobile.

Keywords: accessibility, a11y, wcag, screen reader, keyboard, aria, contrast, disability
Good Story Example: As a keyboard-only user, I want to navigate the entire application using Tab and arrow keys so that I can use the product effectively without relying on a mouse.
Bad Story Example: (Rarely written as a story — treated as done when visuals look fine)
AC Example:
- [ ] All interactive elements reachable via Tab in logical order
- [ ] Focus state visible on all interactive elements (ring outline)
- [ ] All icons used as buttons have aria-label
- [ ] Color is not the sole indicator of state (add icon or text)
- [ ] Screen reader announces dynamic content changes via aria-live
INVEST Issues to Watch: Testable: each criterion is objectively verifiable
SaaS Context Notes: WCAG 2.1 AA is a legal requirement in the EU and increasingly in US federal contracts. Enterprise buyers ask for VPAT.

Keywords: api, developer, sdk, webhook, token, oauth, rate limit, documentation
Good Story Example: As a developer integrating our product, I want clear API error messages with an error code and documentation link so that I can resolve integration issues without filing a support ticket.
Bad Story Example: As a developer, I want an API.
AC Example:
- [ ] API error responses follow the format: {error_code, message, docs_url}
- [ ] Error codes are stable across API versions (not subject to change)
- [ ] Rate limit remaining returned in response headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining)
- Given the developer exceeds the rate limit, when the API is called, then a 429 response is returned with Retry-After header
- [ ] All new endpoints documented in OpenAPI spec before GA
INVEST Issues to Watch: Estimable: error message system vs full API versioning vs SDK are separate stories
SaaS Context Notes: Developer experience IS the product for API-first SaaS. Documentation quality is a direct conversion driver.
